name: Relleu, Grana and Torremanzanas Loop
name_original: Desde Relleu hasta Torremanzanas — Circular por el Barranco de Bartolons
description: This moderate 19.1-mile mountain biking route from Relleu to Torremanzanas takes you through the scenic Barranco de Bartolons with 2703 feet
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Relleu are characterized by rugged mountainous terrain and diverse natural features. The region features prominent peaks such as Cabeçó d'Or and Puig Campana, alongside the scenic Amadorio River valley. This landscape provides a variety of trails, from gravel roads to natural paths, offering varied surfaces for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Relleu The most popular mountain bike trail is Relleu, Grana and Torremanzanas Loop, a 19.1 miles (30.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a comprehensive loop through the area's mountainous landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Historic Center of Orxeta – Comptador Pass loop from Orxeta, a difficult 31.4 miles (50.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and challenging terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the View of Puig Campana – View of the mountain ranges loop from Sella, a 20.4 miles (32.9 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes with views of Puig Campana, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Mountain biking around Relleu is defined by rugged terrain, mountainous landscapes, and the Amadorio River valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Relleu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 1500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Relleu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Relleu?
A: There are over 130 mountain bike trails around Relleu, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 80 moderate routes, and 44 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Relleu?
A: Relleu's mountain bike trails are characterized by rugged mountainous terrain, featuring prominent peaks like Cabeçó d'Or and Puig Campana. You'll encounter a variety of surfaces, from gravel roads to natural paths, often with short climbs and flowing descents. The Amadorio River valley also offers scenic routes through gorges and canyons.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Relleu?
A: Yes, Relleu offers 14 easy mountain bike trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Relleu?
A: The mountain bike trails in Relleu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 80 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, stunning mountainous landscapes, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Relleu?
A: Yes, many routes in Relleu are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927491802/relleu-grana-and-torremanzanas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Relleu, Grana and Torremanzanas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Orxeta – Comptador Pass loop from Orxeta&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging circular option.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking in Relleu?
A: Many trails offer spectacular views of the region's mountains, including Puig Campana. You might also encounter the impressive 18th-century Relleu Dam along the Amadorio River. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/497288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4228929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Relleu is known for its challenging routes, especially around Puig Campana. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Orxeta – Comptador Pass loop from Orxeta&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 50.6 km path with significant elevation gain, requiring very good fitness and riding skills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Relleu?
A: While specific data isn't provided, Relleu's location in Alicante, Spain, suggests that spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities like mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that pass by natural springs or water features?
A: Yes, the region features the scenic Amadorio River valley, and some routes pass by natural springs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32115813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Orxeta – Font del Molí loop from Orxeta&lt;/a&gt; includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4228929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the iconic Puig Campana?
A: Yes, several trails provide excellent views of Puig Campana. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31919391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Puig Campana – View of the mountain ranges loop from Sella&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase this iconic mountain and the surrounding ranges.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike route available in Relleu?
A: Among the listed routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38231018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Orxeta – Comptador Pass loop from Orxeta&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, covering 50.6 km (31.4 miles) and typically taking around 4 hours and 41 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families with children?
A: While the guide focuses on general mountain biking, the presence of 14 easy routes suggests there are options that could be suitable for families. These easier trails typically have less demanding terrain and elevation, making them more accessible for younger riders or those with less experience.
tips: Very picturesque place with beautiful views of El Figueret, a curious cone-shaped rock formation or "volcano" that stands out in the landscape.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is generally classified as moderate, requiring good fitness due to its length and elevation changes. While some sections are manageable, be prepared for varied technical challenges, including rocky paths and potentially steep descents, which might require advanced skills or even dismounting your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Barranco de Bartolons trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and can be challenging. You'll encounter rocky paths, singletrack, and technical sections typical of a 'barranco' (ravine) environment. Expect hairpin bends, rock gardens, and areas that can feel like a steep, shallow riverbed with loose rocks. Some parts may be demanding enough to require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The 'Desde Relleu hasta Torremanzanas — Circular por el Barranco de Bartolons' route typically takes around 3 hours and 6 minutes to complete by mountain bike, depending on your pace and skill level.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers spectacular views of the rural Alicante landscape, including extensive olive and almond groves, and wild mountain scenery. A specific highlight is the View of El Figueret from Barranc de Bartolons, which provides a dramatic backdrop of the natural gorge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail in Relleu?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Relleu is the starting point for this circular route. You should look for public parking options within or on the outskirts of Relleu village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Alicante region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant scenery. Winter can also be a good option, though some sections might be wet after rain.

faqs.question: Is the 'Desde Relleu hasta Torremanzanas — Circular por el Barranco de Bartolons' trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to ensure they are fit enough for the varied and sometimes technical terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route through the Barranco de Bartolons. It's a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. Approximately 27% of the trail follows 'El Camí IT5TR2 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana', and about 26% coincides with the 'Camino del Sureste - Ramal de Benidorm'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, appropriate safety gear (helmet, gloves), plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection is also advisable, especially during sunnier months.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail in Relleu or Torremanzanas?
faqs.answer: Both Relleu and Torremanzanas are small villages that typically offer local cafes or restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check their opening hours, especially if you're riding outside of peak tourist season.

waypoints.name: View of El Figueret from Barranc de Bartolons
